heading: Enable Snapshot Copy Distribution
body:
To enable and configure snapshot copy distribution from your primary
region to other regions using the Atlas UI:

This displays a table where you can configure snapshot copy policy
items to distribute snapshot copies to additional regions.

> **Note:** For a global cluster, you can enable or disable this setting
> independently for each zone.

To copy snapshots to all secondary regions in a multi-region
cluster, toggle Automatically Sync Copy Regions with Cluster Regions
to On.

When you enable Automatically Sync Copy Regions with Cluster
Regions, Atlas configures snapshot copy
policy items for each secondary region in your cluster and keeps
the policy in sync with the cluster configuration as you add or
remove secondary regions. Atlas creates each snapshot copy
policy item with the same frequency and retention times as the
backup policy items defined for the cluster.

You can customize the frequency and retention times for the
automatic snapshot copies in the following steps, but you cannot
add or remove regions unless you disable this setting.

In the Region column of the snapshot copy policy
table, select a region from the Choose a region dropdown.

To add another region, click Add Another Region and
Frequency.

> **Note:** You must select copy regions from within the same cloud provider as
> the primary region of your cluster. For example, if your cluster's
> [highest priority region](manual/reference/glossary) is set to the AWS (Amazon Web Services) region
> `us-east-1`, you can only distribute snapshot copies to other AWS (Amazon Web Services)
> regions supported by Atlas.
>
> If you have a global cluster, each zone has its own primary
> region. You must select copy regions within the same cloud
> provider as the primary region for each zone.

> **Note:** If Automatically Sync Copy Regions with Cluster Regions
> is set to On, you can't add or delete copy regions,
> but you can modify the frequency and retention times for the snapshot
> copy policy items that Atlas automatically configures for each
> secondary region in your cluster.

In the Snapshots column for each region that you
added, select the frequency for copying snapshots to that region.

You can select from the following frequency options:

- Last number of snapshots: Copies a specific number
  of the most recent snapshots to the copy region, deleting older
  snapshots as new snapshots are copied. If you select this
  option, you can't select any other frequency options for that
  region.

  For example, specifying Last `5`
  Snapshots copies the five most recent snapshots
  taken on the source region, and maintains that number of copies
  for as long as the copy policy item is active.

- Backup Policy Frequency: Copies snapshots taken
  according to the [cluster-level backup policy](configure-backup-policy)
  item(s) defined for your cluster. Possible values include
  `Hourly`, `Daily`, `Weekly`, `Monthly`, or `Yearly`.

  The available frequencies correspond to the frequencies of the
  backup policy items defined for your cluster. For example, if
  your backup policy specifies only weekly and monthly snapshots,
  then you can select either `Weekly` or `Monthly` as the
  snapshot copy frequency to copy those respective snapshots to
  the copy region.

  Atlas copies snapshots taken for *all* backup policy items
  with the specified frequency. For example, if your backup policy
  takes two weekly snapshots, then a `Weekly` snapshot copy
  policy item copies both weekly snapshots.

  To select more than one frequency option for the same copy
  region, click Add Another Region and Frequency and
  choose the same region in the new row.

  > **Note:** If overlapping policy items generate the same snapshot, Atlas
  > associates the snapshot with the policy item with the longest retention
  > time.
  >
  > For example, if the policy specifies a daily snapshot with two-day
  > retention and a weekly snapshot every Saturday with three-week
  > retention, then Atlas displays a frequency of Weekly in
  > the Frequency column on the Snapshots page for
  > the snapshot taken on Saturday and retains the snapshot for three weeks.
  > This is because the weekly policy item has a longer retention time than
  > the daily policy item.

- On Demand: Copies on-demand snapshots
  taken for the cluster.

In the Retention Time column for each row that you
added, select the retention time for snapshots copied to that
region and frequency. The retention time cannot exceed the
retention time set

You can choose from the following units of time for retention:

- Hours

- Days

- Weeks

- Months

In the Point-in-Time Restore column for each row that
you added, set the toggle to On to enable oplog copy
distribution to that region. This enables PIT (Point in Time) restores for all
snapshot copies in that region.

This is only available if you enabled Continuous Cloud Backup for your
cluster.

Review the new snapshot copy policy configuration in the dialog
box, and click Confirm to save your changes.
